reasoning: The baseline value as of 2025-08-31 is approximately 5.10. The BBB corporate yield index has been in a strong downtrend over the last few months (falling from 5.77 in April), driven largely by market expectations of multiple Federal Reserve rate cuts beginning in September 2025. Markets currently assign an 85-95% probability to a September rate cut. For the short-term horizons (Sept 7 and Sept 30), downward momentum is expected to persist as we head toward the highly anticipated September FOMC meeting, making an increase less likely (< 40%). However, corporate yields also depend on credit spreads and the term premium. Spreads are currently very tight and have little room to narrow, but could widen materially on economic slowing or tariff implementation risks in late 2025. Additionally, the yield curve may steepen (long-end rates rising) as the Fed cuts short-term rates. These factors increase the likelihood of yields rising in the medium to long term. Thus, for the later resolution dates (Nov 2025 and Feb 2026), uncertainty increases markedly, pulling the forecasted probability closer to the base-rate of 0.50.
